title: strychnos tchibangensis
intro: Strychnos tchibangensis, also known as the Tchibanga or Poisonous fruit, is a plant species native to West Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its high concentration of strychnine, a powerful alkaloid. The health benefits of Strychnos tchibangensis include its potential antipyretic, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Strychnos tchibangensis can be found in various forms, including extracts, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
